title: Secret writer with replace-not-append and env-file startup fallback
action: Work test-first: for each behaviour below, write the test and record its FAILING output before implementing, so the cap carries red-failure evidence. Two things, both in src/config. First, a secret writer per D10: write or update a key in herdr-go.env reusing prepare_token_directory and validate_token_protection for permission handling, and REPLACE an existing key in place rather than appending, since ensure_web_secret today only appends and would otherwise produce duplicate lines. Note that write_new_token (src/config/mod.rs) opens with create_new(true) and refuses an existing path (see test token_file_is_created_owner_only... which asserts AlreadyExists on a second write) - it cannot be reused as-is for a replace. Implement the replace by writing the full new file content (existing lines with the target key's value swapped or appended, all other keys untouched) to a temp file in the same directory with the same mode-600/ACL protection, then atomically renaming it over the existing env file - never call write_new_token directly against a path that may already exist. Second, implement D8: Secrets loading gains a fallback that reads herdr-go.env for keys absent from the process environment. The process environment always wins. validate_token_protection must pass before the file is trusted; a file that fails protection is ignored with a non-fatal diagnostic, never silently used. A missing env file is not an error. This cell changes startup behaviour, so treat the precedence and permission-refusal cases as the primary contract. Scope note: the Secrets struct (src/config/mod.rs:42) currently derives Debug, which would print all three token fields verbatim if any caller ever formats a Secrets value with {:?} - no such call exists today, but this cell's own 'no secret in Debug output' truth is only real if a redacting Debug impl replaces the derive (or the derive is removed and an explicit redacting impl added) as part of this cell, not left as pre-existing risk. Also update the existing secrets_read_from_env_only test (src/config/mod.rs:965-969): once from_env() gains an env-file fallback, its 'process env absent -> field is None' assertion is only true when no herdr-go.env happens to exist on the machine running the test, so it must be rewritten to explicitly control for (or assert against) the env-file fallback path, not left unmodified expecting the same behavior it tested before this cell.

truths: A value present in the process environment always wins over the same key in herdr-go.env.
A herdr-go.env whose permissions fail validate_token_protection is not used as a secret source, and the refusal is visible without printing any secret value.
An absent herdr-go.env is not an error and leaves behaviour as it is today.
Writing a key that already exists in herdr-go.env results in exactly one line for that key, not a duplicate, and every other existing key in the file is preserved unchanged.
Newly created and rewritten env files are owner-only on unix and on Windows via the existing helpers, with no window where a partially-written file is readable at the wrong permissions.
No secret value appears in any log line, error message, or Debug output - Secrets' Debug representation (derived or explicit) never renders a token value.
The existing invariant that tokens are rejected inside config.json still holds.
secrets_read_from_env_only (or its replacement) still correctly documents and tests the env-file fallback's interaction with an empty process environment.

artifacts.substantive: replace-not-append env-file writer (temp-file + atomic rename, never a bare write_new_token against an existing path) plus env-then-file secret resolution gated on permission validation, unit-tested including precedence and refusal cases

key_links: src/config/secrets.rs reuses prepare_token_directory and validate_token_protection from src/config/mod.rs for all permission handling; it does not call write_new_token against a path that may already exist
prohibitions: Do not reimplement file permission or ACL logic beyond what atomic temp-file-then-rename requires; reuse the existing helpers for the actual mode/ACL setting.
Do not call write_new_token directly on a path that may already exist - it will error AlreadyExists.
Do not let the env file override a value already set in the process environment.
Do not trust an env file that fails permission validation.
Do not print, log, or include any secret value in output or error text, or leave Secrets' Debug output capable of rendering a token value.
Do not cause migrate_default_state_if to run in doctor mode, per D12.
Do not prompt or read stdin in this cell.

trace.outcome: Added src/config/secrets.rs: D10 replace-not-append env writer (temp-file + atomic rename over herdr-go.env, owner-only via reused prepare_token_directory/write_new_token/validate_token_protection, never write_new_token on the live path) and D8 env-then-file secret resolution gated on validate_token_protection (process env wins, unprotected file ignored with non-fatal diagnostic, absent file not an error). Redacting Debug impl on Secrets replaces the derive so no {:?} can leak a token. Updated the secrets_read_from_env_only test for the fallback.

secrets.rs read_trusted_env_file: on validate_token_protection Err, eprintln warning with path only (no value); error e is mod.rs:846 "saved web token is not owner-only". Test env_file_failing_protection_is_ignored_not_used (mode 0o640) asserts telegram_bot_token.is_none()

secrets.rs replace_or_append replaces first matching prefix line, drops later dupes, preserves all other lines; test write_secret_replaces_existing_key_and_preserves_others asserts matches count==1, new value present, old gone, other key preserved

secrets.rs write_secret_in writes to fresh tmp file via write_new_token (create_new(true)+mode(0o600)), then atomic rename over env_path; tests write_secret_creates_env_file_owner_only and rewritten_env_file_stays_owner_only assert mode&0o777==0o600
mod.rs manual impl Debug for Secrets redacts each field to <redacted>/<unset>; test secrets_debug_never_renders_a_token_value asserts rendered excludes all three token strings
mod.rs:89 deny_unknown_fields untouched by diff; test token_field_is_rejected_no_such_field still present and passing

secrets.rs write_secret_in calls write_new_token only against a random tmp_path, then renames over env_path
migrate_default_state_if only referenced in src/main.rs (untouched); this cell's diff touches only src/config/mod.rs and src/config/secrets.rs
temp file opens owner-only from first byte (create_new+mode 0o600), rename is atomic, tmp removed on error
